verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B encodes the F1 beta subunit of mitochondrial ATP synthase, a canonical mitochondrial matrix-facing subunit of the inner-membrane ATP synthase complex. The protein is integrated into the inner mitochondrial membrane with its catalytic domain oriented toward the matrix, making it accessible only from the mitochondrial lumen, not from the extracellular face. No documented cell-state-induced ecto-display, tissue-restricted surface expression, lysosomal exocytosis pathway involvement, PM cycling/dual localization, or stable TM-partner surface attachment exists for this canonical metabolic enzyme. Cancer-cell ecto-presentation of mitochondrial enzymes has been documented for glycolytic enzymes and some chaperones, but ATP5F1B is a structural ATP synthase subunit with no reported surface display in transformed cells or during cell death. The protein has no documented secretory pathway stages, shedding, or surface-targeting modifications.
